public_reputation: Master builder overseeing keel laying, timber curing, and apprentice advancement in Lirelith.
appearance: Master Shipwright Belor Finn carries the marks of master shipwright in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries the habits of a master shipwright whose work is visible through daily procedure, gatekeeping, and the practical pressures of Lirelith.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of master shipwright in and around Lirelith, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Lirelith while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Master Shipwright Belor Finn can draw the party into a practical problem centered on Lirelith.
Master Shipwright Belor Finn becomes useful when the party needs access, explanation, or relief tied to the work of a master shipwright.

support_scope_notes: Created from the estate and daily-life infrastructure execution patch to make polity-native estate operations usable at table scale.
public_signal: In public, Master Shipwright Belor Finn is readable as a master shipwright tied to Lirelith.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Lirelith: role-relevant halls, counters, corridors, docks, archives, shrines, workshops, gates, or administrative rooms

first_impression: Master Shipwright Belor Finn presents as someone shaped by the duties of a master shipwright and the daily pressures of Lirelith.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a master shipwright, and proof that strangers will not make Lirelith harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Master Shipwright Belor Finn refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a master shipwright too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Master Shipwright Belor Finn can open local doors, explain expectations, and redirect pressure before it hardens.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Master Shipwright Belor Finn adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Master Shipwright Belor Finn always knows more about Lirelith than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to master shipwright duties in Lirelith.
social_texture: Master Shipwright Belor Finn speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Lirelith, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Master touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Master Shipwright Belor Finn when the party needs a socially grounded face for Lirelith: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
